findings.message: The shared-component contract misstates current checkout behavior. `checkout/DaySelector.tsx` does not render an always-open inline calendar; it keeps an inline `isCalendarOpen` toggle and only renders `DayPicker` when opened. Implementing the plan literally would regress the checkout UI.

The color-variant migration is scoped incorrectly. TASK-05 is the only caller given `calendarColorVariant="warning"`, but current `DateSel.tsx` and `DateSelectorAllo.tsx` also use warning tokens for `today` and `selected`. Migrating loans and Alloggiati on the default primary variant would introduce visible styling regressions on two screens.
TASK-05 drops the existing `username` pass-through even though the plan's non-goals explicitly defer removing deprecated `username` props.
TASK-07 says the `DateSelectorCI` import alias can be kept, but TASK-08's final grep gate requires zero `DateSelectorCI` matches. Those two acceptance paths conflict.
